zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Wood於大約1509年出生在英國素瑞格德斯同時，他的父親Thomas Wood50歲，母親Denes or Diones50歲。他於1525年7月30日與Katherine Stubberd在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特聖 馬丁 在 馮 菲爾茲結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1560年4月18日在英國英格蘭素瑞格德斯同享年52歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素瑞洛瑟爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Wood于大约1509年出生在英国素瑞格德斯同时，他的父亲Thomas Wood50岁，母亲Denes or Diones50岁。他于1525年7月30日与Katherine Stubberd在英国英格兰倫敦維斯特明斯特聖 馬丁 在 馮 菲爾茲结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1560年4月18日在英国英格兰素瑞格德斯同享年52岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素瑞洛瑟爾。

nameMeanings.name: Wood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: mainly a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a wood, from Middle English wode ‘wood’ (Old English wudu). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, such as French Bois and Polish Les .
English: in a few cases, a nickname for an eccentric or perhaps a violent person, from Middle English wode ‘frenzied, wild’ (Old English wōd).

variants.name: Woode
meanings: English: variant of   Wood  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 312, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Woods
meanings: English: variant of Wood with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Irish: adopted as a translation of Ó Cuill ‘descendant of Coll’ (see Quill ), or in Ulster of Mac Con Coille ‘son of Cú Choille’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the wood’, which has also been mistranslated Cox , as if formed with coileach ‘cock, rooster’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Wood was born about 1509, in Godstone, Surrey, England, his father, Thomas Wood, was 50 and his mother, Denes or Diones, was 50. He married Katherine Stubberd on 30 July 1525, in St Martin in the Fields, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 18 April 1560, in Godstone, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52, and was buried in Rotherhithe, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Katherine Stubberd le 30 juillet 1525, à St Martin in the Fields, Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 18 avril 1560, à Godstone, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Rotherhithe, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.St Martin in the Fields, Westminster, Lo: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 스트 마틴 인 테 필드즈
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Wood(이)가 대략 1509년 영국 수리 고드스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Wood(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Denes or Diones(은)는 50세였다. 그는 1525년 7월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 스트 마틴 인 테 필드즈에서 Katherine Stubberd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1560년 4월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 수리 고드스톤에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수리 로더히테에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Wood nasceu aproximadamente 1509, em Godstone, Surrey, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Wood, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Denes or Diones, tinha 50 anos. Ele casou-se com Katherine Stubberd em 30 de julho de 1525, em St Martin in the Fields, Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 18 de abril de 1560, em Godstone, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Rotherhithe, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Wood родился около 1509 г. в местности годстоун, сурри, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Wood было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Denes or Diones, 50. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Stubberd 30 июля 1525 г. в местности ст мартинь ин т фиелдс, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 18 апреля 1560 г. в местности годстоун, сурри, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности ротирит, сурри, Англия, Великобритания.
